children.heading: Nondisclosure clause
children.label: 1 Nondisclosure clause
children.text: The term “nondisclosure clause” means a provision in a contract or agreement that requires the parties to the contract or agreement not to disclose or discuss conduct, the existence of a settlement involving conduct, or information covered by the terms and conditions of the contract or agreement.

children.heading: Nondisparagement clause
children.label: 2 Nondisparagement clause
children.text: The term “nondisparagement clause” means a provision in a contract or agreement that requires 1 or more parties to the contract or agreement not to make a negative statement about another party that relates to the contract, agreement, claim, or case.

children.heading: Sexual assault dispute
children.label: 3 Sexual assault dispute
children.text: The term “sexual assault dispute” means a dispute involving a nonconsensual sexual act or sexual contact, as such terms are defined in or similar applicable Tribal or State law, including when the victim lacks capacity to consent.section 2246 of title 18

children.heading: Sexual harassment dispute
children.label: 4 Sexual harassment dispute
children.text: The term “sexual harassment dispute” means a dispute relating to conduct that is alleged to constitute sexual harassment under applicable Federal, Tribal, or State law.
